perItemFindings.label: transition-mediated-converse-first-order-bracket
perItemFindings.one_line: Delivered tier subfield < floor field; directed reroute: Replace the first-order upper witness with a rank-stable cross-fitted higher-order estimator and prove uniform projection, transport-remainder, operator, and variance bounds yielding risk at most C{n^{-1/2}+n^{-r_1}+n^{-r_2}} over the same model. This would close the bracket and establish the root-n if-and-only-if elbow.

review.verbatim_critique:
[D0.5.G cold referee] delivered tier=subfield below novelty_target=field (floor=field). The main theorem delivers two lower channels and a standard first-order upper bound whose exponents can be widely separated, so it establishes neither the minimax rate nor a sharp root-n boundary. In the first fuzzy submodel, the second treatment and terminal regression are fixed, and the transition merely encodes a t-smooth conditional regression for a one-time missing-at-random mean problem; consequently the r_1 construction is a narrow longitudinal embedding of the classical semiparametric fuzzy-mixture mechanism rather than a new general minimax phenomenon. The deterministic correction staircase and Gram-rank diagnostics supply none of the stochastic higher-order analysis needed to improve the upper bound. The TL;DR, introduction, and scope statements correctly leave the frontier open, so there is no stale claim of a matched or sharp result.

DIRECTED IMPROVEMENT (re-derive the SAME object implementing this, do not reproduce the prior note): Replace the first-order upper witness with a rank-stable cross-fitted higher-order estimator and prove uniform projection, transport-remainder, operator, and variance bounds yielding risk at most C{n^{-1/2}+n^{-r_1}+n^{-r_2}} over the same model. This would close the bracket and establish the root-n if-and-only-if elbow.
